questions.text: How often is GreenOps input used in real decisions rather than just reporting?

options.label: GreenOps data not used in any real decisions. Reporting only, if that.
Occasionally referenced in sustainability reports or board packs. Not part of operational decisions.
Used in some planning and investment discussions. Influences direction but not formally embedded.
Routinely used in procurement, capacity planning and operational decisions. Part of business cases.
Embedded in governance: investment approval, procurement criteria, capacity planning, supplier management and operational review.

questions.text: Can you link GreenOps insights to named owners and actions?

options.label: No link between GreenOps insights and named decision-makers or actions.
Insights shared with sustainability team but not linked to operational owners.
Main insights linked to infrastructure or operations leads. Actions sometimes follow.
Insights linked to named owners with expected actions. Progress tracked for major items.
Full accountability: every insight has a named owner, expected action, timeline and tracked outcome.

questions.text: How mature is the workflow for turning insight into optimisation activity?

options.label: No workflow. Insight exists in reports that nobody acts on.
Ad-hoc: someone spots an issue and raises it informally.
Defined process for major optimisation actions (e.g. rightsizing, decommission). Not consistent.
Structured workflow: identify, assess, approve, implement, verify. Active for most optimisation types.
Automated workflow embedded in operational processes. Actions triggered, tracked and verified against expected outcomes.

questions.text: How much of the estate or organisation is covered by this decision process?

options.label: Decision process covers none of the estate.
Covers one or two areas (e.g. main data centre, largest cloud account).
Covers main on-premise and cloud environments. Colo and edge less integrated.
Covers most of the estate including on-premise, cloud and major colo.
Comprehensive coverage across all environments. No material scope excluded from decision processes.

questions.text: How quickly can teams act on GreenOps signals?

options.label: Teams cannot act on GreenOps signals — data is too old, too broad or not trusted.
Actions possible but slow. Weeks or months between insight and response.
Main teams can act within days or weeks for major issues.
Most teams can act within days. Operational alerts trigger prompt response.
Near-real-time response capability. Automated alerts, pre-approved actions and rapid escalation paths.

questions.text: Can current workflows support time- or location-sensitive decisions where needed?

options.label: Cannot support time or location-sensitive decisions.
Aware that timing and location matter but workflows don't support it.
Some time or location sensitivity in major decisions (e.g. region selection, timing of batch jobs).
Time and location factors built into decision workflows for main scope.
Full temporal and location awareness: carbon-aware scheduling, region-optimised placement, time-of-use decisions standard practice.

questions.text: Do you measure whether actions actually delivered the expected outcome?

options.label: No measurement of whether actions delivered expected outcomes.
Outcomes occasionally reviewed but not systematically tracked.
Outcomes tracked for major actions (e.g. did the decommission reduce energy as expected?).
Systematic outcome tracking for most actions. Variance analysis performed. Lessons captured.
Closed-loop verification: every action tracked against expected outcome. Variance triggers investigation. Continuous improvement embedded.

questions.text: Is it fair to say your GreenOps data is truly decision-grade today?

options.label: No — data is not trusted for decisions.
Partially — data supports directional awareness but not confident decisions.
For some domains — data supports planning and prioritisation. Not yet operational for all areas.
For most domains — data supports procurement, operational and investment decisions with reasonable confidence.
Yes — data is decision-grade across the board. Trusted, timely, granular and embedded in governance.
